ucs.title: [LLM] Malteiro/Mispadu loader chain — VBS/MSI script host invoking certutil/bitsadmin/PowerShell downloader
ucs.description: Hunts the signature Mispadu MaaS infection chain: a Spanish/Portuguese invoice-themed phish drops an MSI or ZIP containing a VBScript (T1059.005) that, once user-executed (T1204.002), spawns a LOLBin downloader (certutil/bitsadmin/curl/PowerShell) to pull the second-stage encrypted payload (T1027.013/T1140). This script-host → downloader transition is the most consistent telemetry artefact across SCILabs and Metabase Q reporting on Malteiro.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_path) as image values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmdline from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","msiexec.exe") OR Processes.parent_process IN ("*.vbs*","*.vbe*","*.js*","*.hta*","*.msi*")) AND Processes.process_name IN ("certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") AND (Processes.process="*-urlcache*" OR Processes.process="*-split*" OR Processes.process="*/transfer*" OR Processes.process="*DownloadFile*" OR Processes.process="*DownloadString*" OR Processes.process="*Invoke-WebRequest*" OR Processes.process="*-OutFile*" OR Processes.process="*http://*" OR Processes.process="*https://*") AND NOT Processes.user IN ("*$","SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
// Malteiro/Mispadu first-stage downloader: VBS or MSI invokes a LOLBin to fetch the encrypted second stage
let _script_hosts = dynamic(["wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","msiexec.exe"]);
let _downloaders  = dynamic(["certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe"]);
let _download_args = dynamic(["-urlcache","-split","-f","/transfer","DownloadFile","DownloadString","Invoke-WebRequest","-OutFile","http://","https://"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (_script_hosts)
| where FileName in~ (_downloaders)
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (_download_args)
// Mispadu also script-pivots through the user's profile / Public folder
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (@"\Users\", @"\Public\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\Windows\Temp\")
   or InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any (".vbs",".vbe",".js",".hta",".msi")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ParentPath = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          Downloader = FileName,
          DownloaderCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          GrandParent = InitiatingProcessParentFileName,
          SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Malteiro/Mispadu first-stage downloader on Sentinel via 4688
let _script_hosts = dynamic(["wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","msiexec.exe"]);
let _downloaders  = dynamic(["certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe"]);
let _download_args = dynamic(["-urlcache","-split","/transfer","DownloadFile","DownloadString","Invoke-WebRequest","-OutFile","http://","https://"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend ParentBin = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName,"\\")[-1])),
         NewBin    = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName,"\\")[-1]))
| where ParentBin in (_script_hosts)
| where NewBin in (_downloaders)
| where CommandLine has_any (_download_args)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Malteiro/Mispadu Loader Chain - Script Host Spawns LOLBin Downloader
id: 7c3f9c9a-8a6d-4f1f-9d6e-2b1a8e64a1bc
status: experimental
description: Detects the Mispadu/Malteiro initial-access loader pattern where a VBS, HTA, JS, or MSI launched by the user spawns certutil, bitsadmin, curl, or PowerShell to download the encrypted second-stage payload.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G1026/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/software/S1122/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/04
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1566.001
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.t1059.005
  - attack.g1026
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\msiexec.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\curl.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
  selection_args:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '-urlcache'
      - '-split'
      - '/transfer'
      - 'DownloadFile'
      - 'DownloadString'
      - 'Invoke-WebRequest'
      - '-OutFile'
      - 'http://'
      - 'https://'
  filter_machine:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child and selection_args and not filter_machine
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate software installers using msiexec to fetch components
  - Admin scripting via cscript invoking certutil for cert pinning operations
level: high
ucs.rationale: Malteiro/Mispadu's MaaS chain has been remarkably stable since 2019: invoice-lure HTML/ZIP/MSI → user opens → embedded VBS (T1059.005) → LOLBin download of an encrypted blob (T1027.013) that AutoIt later decodes (T1140). Script-host -> certutil/bitsadmin is rare in benign use, and combining the parent (scripting host or msiexec) with a downloader child plus URL-fetch arguments produces a tight det

ucs.title: [LLM] Malteiro/Mispadu browser credential exfil — non-browser process touching Chromium 'Login Data' / Firefox logins.json
ucs.description: Hunts Mispadu's banking-trojan credential-theft stage: after AutoIt loader execution, a non-browser binary (often a renamed AutoIt3.exe or VBS-spawned helper) reads or copies Chromium 'Login Data' SQLite, Firefox 'logins.json'/'key4.db', and Outlook profile files to harvest stored passwords (T1555.003) before exfil to the actor's C2. The combination of these specific file targets being touched by a non-browser, non-indexer process is high-signal for Malteiro post-exploitation.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as paths values(Filesystem.process_name) as procs values(Filesystem.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_name IN ("Login Data","Login Data For Account","logins.json","key4.db","key3.db","cookies.sqlite","Web Data") OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Google\\Chrome\\User Data\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Microsoft\\Edge\\User Data\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Mozilla\\Firefox\\Profiles\\*") AND NOT Filesystem.process_name IN ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","explorer.exe","SearchProtocolHost.exe","SearchFilterHost.exe","MsMpEng.exe","backgroundtaskhost.exe","OneDrive.exe") by host Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.process_path Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.user | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | where NOT match(user,"(?i)\$$|^SYSTEM$") | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
// Malteiro/Mispadu credential-store harvest: non-browser process accessing Chrome/Edge/Firefox secrets
let _cred_files = dynamic(["Login Data","Login Data For Account","logins.json","key4.db","key3.db","cookies.sqlite","Web Data","Cookies"]);
let _browser_paths = dynamic([@"\Google\Chrome\User Data\", @"\Microsoft\Edge\User Data\", @"\Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles\", @"\BraveSoftware\Brave-Browser\User Data\"]);
let _benign_procs = dynamic(["chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","explorer.exe","searchprotocolhost.exe","searchfilterhost.exe","msmpeng.exe","mssense.exe","backgroundtaskhost.exe","onedrive.exe","code.exe"]);
// 1. File-side: copy/rename of credential store by non-browser process (Mispadu stages Login Data to %TEMP% before reading)
let FileTouch = DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed","FileCopied")
    | where (FileName in~ (_cred_files) or PreviousFileName in~ (_cred_files))
         or (FolderPath has_any (_browser_paths) or PreviousFolderPath has_any (_browser_paths))
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (_benign_procs)
    | where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
              ActorProcess = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              ActorPath    = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
              ActorCmd     = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              ActorSHA256  = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
              TouchedFile  = FileName,
              TouchedPath  = FolderPath,
              ActionType,
              Source = "FileEvent";
// 2. Process-side: command line directly references the credential file (sqlite3, powershell, autoit cases)
let CmdTouch = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("Login Data","logins.json","key4.db","cookies.sqlite",@"\Chrome\User Data\",@"\Edge\User Data\",@"\Firefox\Profiles\")
    | where FileName !in~ (_benign_procs)
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
              ActorProcess = FileName,
              ActorPath    = FolderPath,
              ActorCmd     = ProcessCommandLine,
              ActorSHA256  = SHA256,
              TouchedFile  = "",
              TouchedPath  = "",
              ActionType   = "ProcessRef",
              Source = "ProcessEvent";
union FileTouch, CmdTouch
// Strong Malteiro pivot: actor binary lives in user-writable path, common Mispadu staging area
| extend SuspiciousLocation = ActorPath has_any (@"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Windows\Temp\")
| extend AutoItSuspect = ActorProcess has_any ("autoit","au3") or ActorCmd has_any (".a3x","AutoIt3")
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Malteiro/Mispadu credential-store harvest on Sentinel via ASIM file events
let _cred_files = dynamic(["Login Data","Login Data For Account","logins.json","key4.db","key3.db","cookies.sqlite","Web Data","Cookies"]);
let _browser_paths = dynamic([@"\Google\Chrome\User Data\", @"\Microsoft\Edge\User Data\", @"\Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles\", @"\BraveSoftware\Brave-Browser\User Data\"]);
let _benign_procs = dynamic(["chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","explorer.exe","searchprotocolhost.exe","searchfilterhost.exe","msmpeng.exe","mssense.exe","backgroundtaskhost.exe","onedrive.exe"]);
ImFileEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
| where (TargetFileName in~ (_cred_files) or TargetFilePath has_any (_browser_paths))
| extend ActorBin = tolower(tostring(split(ActingProcessName,"\\")[-1]))
| where ActorBin !in (_benign_procs)
| where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
| extend SuspiciousLocation = ActingProcessName has_any (@"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\ProgramData\")
| extend AutoItSuspect = ActingProcessName has_any ("autoit","au3") or ActingProcessCommandLine has_any (".a3x","AutoIt3")
| project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
          ActingProcessName, ActingProcessCommandLine,
          TargetFileName, TargetFilePath, EventType,
          SuspiciousLocation, AutoItSuspect
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Malteiro/Mispadu Browser Credential Store Access by Non-Browser Process
id: e9d4b2af-4d7c-4a1d-bb6f-1f3a7c0e5d22
status: experimental
description: Detects Mispadu banking-trojan credential theft - non-browser/non-indexer processes opening or copying Chromium 'Login Data', Firefox 'logins.json'/'key4.db', or related browser secret stores. Mispadu typically uses an AutoIt loader running from %TEMP% or %APPDATA% to perform this harvest.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G1026/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/software/S1122/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/04
tags:
  - attack.credential_access
  - attack.t1555
  - attack.t1555.003
  - attack.g1026
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_target_file:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '\Login Data'
      - '\Login Data For Account'
      - '\logins.json'
      - '\key4.db'
      - '\key3.db'
      - '\cookies.sqlite'
      - '\Web Data'
  selection_target_path:
    TargetFilename|contains:
      - '\Google\Chrome\User Data\'
      - '\Microsoft\Edge\User Data\'
      - '\Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles\'
      - '\BraveSoftware\Brave-Browser\User Data\'
  filter_browsers:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\chrome.exe'
      - '\msedge.exe'
      - '\firefox.exe'
      - '\brave.exe'
      - '\explorer.exe'
      - '\SearchProtocolHost.exe'
      - '\SearchFilterHost.exe'
      - '\MsMpEng.exe'
      - '\MsSense.exe'
      - '\OneDrive.exe'
  filter_system:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: (selection_target_file or selection_target_path) and not filter_browsers and not filter_system
falsepositives:
  - Password manager imports (1Password, Bitwarden, KeePassXC) - allowlist these vendors
  - Backup tools that snapshot user profile directories
  - Browser-extension installers in enterprise deployments
level: high
ucs.rationale: Malteiro is overwhelmingly financially motivated (T1657) and Mispadu's primary monetisation is harvested banking/email credentials from LATAM and Iberian victims. Where most APTs lean on LSASS dumping or Kerberoasting, Malteiro's documented tradecraft is consistently browser-store + Outlook profile theft via AutoIt-compiled loaders dropped to %TEMP%/%APPDATA% (per ESET, SCILabs, and Metabase Q rep
